title: My grandmother's hands
summary: Describe your grandmother's hands and one small memory.
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about your grandmother's hands and one memory. Use possessive 's and simple past for one short story.
hintStarters: She often used her hands to…
sampleText: My grandmother lived in a small village in southern Italy, and her hands tell her story. Her hands are small but strong. The skin is thin and warm, and you can see the blue lines under it. She wears one gold ring on her left hand. She has worked with her hands for seventy years. She sews shirts, she knits scarves, and she rolls fresh pasta on a wooden table every Sunday. Her hands know the kitchen better than her eyes. I remember one cold winter day from my childhood. I was six, and I had a fever. My grandmother sat next to my bed, and she held my hand for a long time. Her hand was warm, and the skin was soft. She did not say many words. She only sang a quiet song from her village. Her hands made me feel safe. Now she is eighty-two, and her hands move more slowly. I take her hand every time I visit, because I never want to forget how it feels.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Possessive 's for family
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 's sau tên/người để chỉ sở hữu: 'My grandmother's hands', 'her grandmother's village'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 's after a person to show possession, like 'My grandmother's hands'.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Simple past for a memory
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng quá khứ đơn để kể một kỷ niệm: 'sat', 'held', 'sang'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the simple past for a memory, as in 'she sat next to my bed' and 'she held my hand'.

sampleTranslationVi: Bà tôi sống ở một ngôi làng nhỏ phía nam nước Ý, và đôi tay bà kể câu chuyện của bà. Tay bà nhỏ nhưng mạnh. Da mỏng và ấm, có thể thấy những đường gân xanh phía dưới. Bà đeo một chiếc nhẫn vàng ở tay trái. Bà đã làm việc bằng đôi tay này suốt bảy mươi năm. Bà may áo, đan khăn và cán mì tươi trên một chiếc bàn gỗ mỗi Chủ nhật. Đôi tay bà thuộc bếp hơn cả mắt bà. Tôi nhớ một ngày đông lạnh thời thơ ấu. Khi đó tôi sáu tuổi và bị sốt. Bà ngồi cạnh giường tôi và nắm tay tôi rất lâu. Tay bà ấm, da mềm. Bà không nói nhiều lời. Bà chỉ hát một bài hát nhẹ từ làng bà. Đôi tay bà làm tôi cảm thấy an toàn. Bây giờ bà tám mươi hai tuổi và tay bà cử động chậm hơn. Tôi nắm tay bà mỗi lần tới thăm, vì tôi không bao giờ muốn quên cảm giác ấy.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses simple past correctly?
options: She sit next to my bed.
She sat next to my bed.
She is sit next to my bed.
She sitting next to my bed yesterday.
Her hands are small but strong.
Hands her but strong small are.
I remember one cold winter day.
The shop opens at nine a.m.
mcQuestions.hint: Pick the sentence about the past.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses simple past correctly?
options: She held my hand for a long time.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which word fits: 'The skin on her hand is ___ and warm.'
mcQuestions.hint: Choose a word that describes skin.

options: I take her hand because I never want to forget.
Because hand take I want forget.
I take because hand forget never.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for a complete reason clause.

options: She is old, but her hands are still kind.
But she old kind hands and.
She hands old and but kind.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for a clean contrast linker.
Adverb of manner after the verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence fits a family description best?
options: She wears one gold ring on her left hand.
The road is muddy after rain.
mcQuestions.explanation: A personal detail fits a family description.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do small physical details — like a person's hands — often hold our strongest memories of them?
